title: External Flow (III) TrendMap
subtitle: Fibonacci Retracement + VWAP Confluence — Signal via MQL5-Python Bridge
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — Fibonacci + VWAP confluence framework and swing high/low detection. The signal logic reveals a powerful idea: VWAP relative to Fibonacci midpoint = directional bias.

series_evolution.part_9: Bridge established. Signal = close vs 10-day average (very weak)
series_evolution.part_10: VWAP replaces naive average. Signal = close vs VWAP. S/R added.
series_evolution.part_12: Major upgrade: Fibonacci levels added alongside VWAP. Signal = VWAP position relative to Fibonacci 50% midpoint. Swing high/low detection introduced. This is where the series starts building real structural analysis.

core_concept.name: VWAP Position Relative to Fibonacci Midpoint
core_concept.description: If VWAP sits BELOW the 50% Fibonacci level (midpoint between swing H and L), the market has a bullish bias — buy when price approaches the 38.2% support. If VWAP is ABOVE the midpoint, bearish bias — sell when price approaches 61.8% resistance.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: The Fibonacci + VWAP confluence concept is the bridge between price memory (Fib) and fair value (VWAP). This is our zone identification problem: where did institutional buying/selling occur and is the market still biased in that direction? Our ZoneIdentifier (supply/demand) is the structural equivalent — more precise than Fibonacci but same concept.

data_requirements.name: h1_ohlcv_100_bars
data_requirements.note: For swing high/low detection and VWAP calculation

data_requirements.name: swing_high_low
data_requirements.method: highest high and lowest low in the lookback window

num_bars.description: Lookback bars for swing detection and VWAP

signal_logic.bias: BULLISH if vwap < midpoint  |  BEARISH if vwap > midpoint
signal_logic.buy_trigger: bias=BULLISH AND current_close approaches fib_38.2 (support zone)
signal_logic.sell_trigger: bias=BEARISH AND current_close approaches fib_61.8 (resistance zone)
signal_logic.note: Approaches = price within tolerance of the level, not exact touch

steps.name: detect_swing_hl
steps.description: Find swing high and low over num_bars lookback

steps.name: calculate_fibonacci_levels
steps.description: Derive all 7 Fibonacci retracement levels from swing H/L

steps.name: calculate_vwap
steps.description: Compute VWAP over the same lookback window

steps.name: determine_bias
steps.description: Compare VWAP to midpoint (50% Fibonacci level)

steps.name: evaluate_entry_zone
steps.description: Check if price is near the key Fibonacci level for the bias direction
steps.buy_zone: close near fib_38.2 (support) when bias is bullish
steps.sell_zone: close near fib_61.8 (resistance) when bias is bearish

steps.name: send_to_python
steps.description: Serialise swing H/L, Fibonacci levels, VWAP, price data as JSON, POST to Flask

steps.name: return_signal
steps.description: Python returns signal JSON, MQL5 draws Fibonacci lines, VWAP line, and signal arrow

chart_objects: Horizontal lines for all 7 Fibonacci levels (blue dotted, labelled)
Buy arrow (green) or Sell arrow (red) at signal

components_to_build.name: FibonacciCalculator
components_to_build.note: Pure function. Returns dict: {'0%': price, '23.6%': price, ...}

components_to_build.name: SwingHighLowDetector
components_to_build.note: Simple: swing_high=max(highs[-lookback:]), swing_low=min(lows[-lookback:]). No fractal needed.

keep: VWAP vs Fibonacci midpoint bias framework — elegant: one number (VWAP relative to 50%) gives directional bias
38.2% as support, 61.8% as resistance for entry zones — textbook Fibonacci entries
Fibonacci level derivation formulas — correct and standard
Swing H/L as Fibonacci anchors — correct application (measure from recent significant range)
JSON response including all levels — machine-parseable, useful for downstream tools
discard: The HTTP bridge — Python-native bypasses this
Simple swing H/L = window max/min — doesn't detect fractal swings, just absolute extremes
38.2% touch as trigger without structural confirmation — price can touch a Fib level many times without reversing
Matplotlib file output — we use our ChartRenderer
improve: Replace window max/min swing detection with our fractal-based StructureEngine swings — more structurally meaningful
Replace 38.2%/61.8% touch with ZONE TOUCH — supply/demand zones near those Fib levels are the real signal
Add VWV gate — is there velocity at the Fibonacci level? Touches without energy are traps
Add multi-timeframe Fibonacci: H4 Fib gives the major levels, H1 Fib gives the entry zone
Add trend confirmation: StructureEngine.market_structure must align with Fibonacci bias
Replace simple VWAP with session-anchored VWAP for intraday trading

creviaderiv_insights.fib_50_as_bias_filter: VWAP vs Fibonacci 50% is a clean bias filter. We can add this: if VWAP > zone_mid (midpoint of our demand/supply zone), bias = bullish. Combines our zone-based structure with VWAP fair value — superior to either alone.
creviaderiv_insights.fib_zones_vs_sd_zones: Fibonacci levels (38.2%, 61.8%) and our supply/demand zones serve the same purpose: mark where institutional activity concentrated. Our zones are derived from actual price structure (impulsive/corrective moves), making them more precise than mechanical Fibonacci ratios. But when Fibonacci levels COINCIDE with our zones, that's a confluence signal worth extra weight.
creviaderiv_insights.swing_hl_improvement: This tool uses window max/min for swing H/L. Our StructureEngine detects fractal swings with proper HH/HL/LH/LL classification. Our swing detection is strictly more sophisticated.

article_figures.title: Fibonacci + VWAP Interaction
article_figures.insight: Shows VWAP line intersecting Fibonacci levels. When VWAP is near 38.2% = bullish zone; near 61.8% = bearish zone.

article_figures.title: System Flowchart
article_figures.insight: Same MQL5-Python bridge as Parts 9-10. Data flow identical, only the Python analysis differs.

article_figures.title: Matplotlib Fibonacci + VWAP Plot
article_figures.insight: Python-generated chart showing price, all 7 Fibonacci levels as horizontal lines, VWAP as curved line. Useful visualization format.

article_figures.title: Live Test on Boom 300 Index (GIF)
article_figures.insight: M1 chart with signal arrows aligned to Fibonacci support zones. Shows signals appearing at 38.2% level bounces.
